titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Spain ( (Bowers), ), Then in France, to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
St. Jean de Luz , December 6, 1937—7 p.m.
[Received December 6—6:15 p.m.]

div.p: 407. My telegram No. 401, November 26, 1 p.m.Not printed. Have just received a letter dated the second from SangronizChief of the Diplomatic Cabinet of the Burgos government. acknowledging in behalf of Franco my letter to him and that “I am today getting in touch with the competent authorities informing them of the contents of your above-mentioned letter”.

He continues, “For the purpose of making absolutely clear everything relative to the nationality of the interested parties I would appreciate your informing me if, on becoming naturalized in the United States, they had inscribed the pertinent notation of their birthplace in the Civil Register directly or through the Spanish Consul of the place where they became naturalized”.

We assume here that they presented to the court directly either their birth certificate or christening certificate as proof of their birthplace. However, I am submitting the question to the Department.

Since the Secretary’s statement on Bilbao ConsulateSee Department’s telegrams to the Ambassador in Spain, Nos. B–378, B–382, and B–384, November 15, 19, and 22, pp. 449, 454, 455. there is evidence that there is some apprehension in diplomatic cabinet lest they have gone too far and that they may relent.
My reply to Sangroniz will be addressed to him personally and signed as you instructed in the case of Franco.
